title: Spec Trace Spec File Custom Editor
purpose: Define the browser-safe custom editor for canonical Spec Trace specification files.
scope: This specification covers opening, viewing, editing, validating, and saving canonical specification JSON files in a VS Code web extension. Custom commands, documentation-focused extensions, language-specific adapters, code lenses, repository automation, and non-spec artifact editors are out of scope for this initial pass.
context: The first extension deliverable should let authors edit existing specification files directly in browser-hosted VS Code without relying on the C# Workbench runtime or any other local helper process.

requirements.title: Run in the web extension host
requirements.statement: The extension MUST run in the VS Code web extension host without requiring Node-only APIs, native binaries, or a separate local helper service.

requirements.title: Register a structured custom editor
requirements.statement: The extension MUST register a structured custom editor for supported canonical specification files so those files open in the structured editor by default, while the standard text editor remains available through VS Code's reopen flow.

requirements.title: Recognize canonical specification files
requirements.statement: The editor MUST treat a file as a canonical specification only when its repository-relative path is within the repository's specification area and its JSON content declares `artifact_type` as `specification` and conforms to the canonical specification shape.
notes: This keeps non-spec JSON files from being opened as spec artifacts.

requirements.title: Confine repository access
requirements.statement: The editor MUST confine file discovery, loading, and saving to the current workspace repository root.

requirements.title: Expose document-level fields
requirements.statement: The editor MUST display `artifact_id` and `artifact_type` and allow editing of `title`, `domain`, `capability`, `status`, `owner`, `purpose`, `scope`, `context`, `tags`, `related_artifacts`, `open_questions`, and `supplemental_sections`.

requirements.title: Edit requirement ordering
requirements.statement: The editor MUST display requirement records in file order and allow the user to add, remove, and reorder them.

requirements.title: Edit requirement fields
requirements.statement: The editor MUST allow editing of each requirement's `id`, `title`, `statement`, `coverage`, `trace`, and `notes` fields.

requirements.title: Preserve schema-authorized extension data
requirements.statement: The editor MUST preserve schema-authorized extension fields, including `x_...` fields, that the user does not modify.
notes: Round-tripping should not discard untouched valid data.

requirements.title: Surface validation while editing
requirements.statement: The editor MUST surface schema and requirement-grammar validation issues while the user edits the document.

requirements.title: Block invalid saves
requirements.statement: The editor MUST block a save when the current draft would violate the canonical specification schema or requirement grammar.

requirements.title: Write deterministic JSON
requirements.statement: The editor MUST write saved specification files as deterministic, human-readable JSON.

requirements.title: Support text-editor fallback
requirements.statement: The editor MUST let the user reopen the same file as plain text without altering the saved artifact.

requirements.title: Detect external file changes
requirements.statement: The editor MUST detect external file changes and prevent silent overwrite of newer content.

requirements.title: Integrate undo and redo
requirements.statement: The editor MUST map user edits into standard VS Code undo and redo behavior.

requirements.title: Prefer VS Code-native controls
requirements.statement: The editor MUST use VS Code-provided form controls when they can represent the required value shape and interaction without loss of fidelity.
notes: This keeps the initial editor lightweight and avoids custom UI unless it is actually needed.

requirements.title: Use @incursa/ui-kit for custom controls
requirements.statement: When VS Code-provided controls cannot represent a required editing interaction, the editor MUST use browser-safe custom controls from the `@incursa/ui-kit` package rather than introducing ad hoc bespoke widgets.
notes: This requirement applies only to custom interactions; it does not force ui-kit usage for fields that native VS Code controls already cover.

requirements.title: Match controls to field semantics
requirements.statement: The editor MUST render each editable field with a control that matches the underlying data semantics, including single-line text entry, multiline text entry, enumerated selection, boolean toggles, repeatable item editors, and ordered record editors.

requirements.title: Surface validation at the smallest applicable scope
requirements.statement: The editor MUST present validation failures at the smallest relevant scope, using inline field feedback for field-local problems and document-level feedback for cross-field or document-shape problems.

requirements.title: Present a derived coverage summary
requirements.statement: The editor MUST present a derived coverage summary for the specification that classifies each requirement as Covered when it has at least one `coverage` entry, Partial when it has no `coverage` entries but does have `trace` or `notes` entries, and Missing when it has no `coverage`, `trace`, or `notes` entries.
notes: The coverage summary is an editor view over existing requirement fields and does not add new persisted document data.

requirements.title: Support coverage drill-down
requirements.statement: The editor MUST let the user drill into a requirement from the coverage summary and reveal that requirement's coverage, trace, and notes details.
